verbGlossary.MODELED IN: {source} geometry and spatial boundaries authored inside a BIM {target}
verbGlossary.ASSEMBLED INTO: {source} serves as an organizing container within a federated multi-discipline {target}
verbGlossary.SEQUENCED IN: {source} tied to a construction sequence phase within a 4D schedule-linked {target}
verbGlossary.VERIFIED AGAINST: As-built conditions at {target} compared to the record model for accuracy
verbGlossary.BOUNDED BY: {source} extent defined by enclosing {target} elements such as walls, slabs, and ceilings
verbGlossary.REFERENCED IN: {source} identifier stored as a parameter or property within {target} element data
verbGlossary.TESTED FOR CLASH IN: Elements within {target} checked for spatial interference via automated clash detection
verbGlossary.SURVEYED BY: {source} condition captured via drone aerial imagery for progress or earthwork tracking
verbGlossary.CAPTURED BY: {source} condition recorded within a 360-degree spherical photo walkthrough
verbGlossary.SCANNED BY: {source} geometry captured as point-cloud data via laser scanning
verbGlossary.MONITORED VIA: {source} systems and equipment streamed to a digital twin for operational tracking
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTED BY: {source} installation progress recorded with a construction photograph
verbGlossary.DEPICTED ON: {source} boundaries and annotations shown on {target}-based drawing or plan sheet
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source} area, volume, or perimeter extracted through quantity takeoff from {target}
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: {source}-based metric statistically linked to {target}-based coordination metric
